description: The test named 'ArrowDown wraps from last to no highlight on second wrap' (FeedRow.dom.vitest.ts:569) has a misleading name. The name suggests that ArrowDown wrapping eventually results in a 'no highlight' state, implying behavior where continuous wrapping might reset the highlight to −1. However, the test body never calls assertNoHighlightedItem and never verifies any 'no highlight' state — it only asserts positive highlights (react, rest, react) through repeated wrapping. The ArrowDown modulo logic (line 139) always produces a valid index and never results in 'no highlight', making the test name inaccurate. This reduces test readability and could mislead future maintainers about the intended wrapping behavior.
expected: Test name should accurately reflect the behavior being tested, such as 'ArrowDown wraps repeatedly through suggestions' or 'ArrowDown continuous wrapping stays within bounds'.
actual: Test name says 'wraps from last to no highlight on second wrap' but the test never asserts any no-highlight state. The test only verifies that the modulo wrapping continues to produce valid highlighted items across multiple full cycles.
recommendation: Rename the test to accurately describe what it verifies. Suggested names: 'ArrowDown continuous wrapping stays within bounds across multiple cycles' or 'ArrowDown wraps repeatedly through all suggestions without errors'.
